test_cases.description: Verify that Run queries SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users to determine boot type before any other action.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened via db.OpenMemory with schema applied (users and admin_config tables exist)
BootstrapParams populated with a valid AdminEmail and a temp ConfigDir
test_cases.expected: Run internally issues SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users as its first database operation; the zero-user branch is executed and Run returns nil
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; row = db.QueryRow('SELECT value FROM admin_config WHERE key = "admin_email"'); assert row.Scan(&v) == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that Run classifies the startup as first boot and executes the first boot sequence when the users table has zero rows.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains zero rows
test_cases.expected: Run returns nil and admin_email is stored in admin_config, indicating the first boot sequence ran
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; v = queryAdminConfig(db, 'admin_email'); assert v == 'admin@example.com'

test_cases.description: Verify that Run classifies the startup as a subsequent boot and executes the subsequent boot sequence when the users table has at least one row.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains one or more rows
admin_config contains a valid admin_token_hash matching ADMIN_TOKEN env var
admin_token file does not exist in ConfigDir
ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable set to a known token whose SHA-256 hash matches admin_token_hash
test_cases.expected: Run returns nil without executing the first boot sequence (no new token generated, admin_email not overwritten)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); token = 'ak_admin_' + hex(randomBytes(32)); hash = hexSHA256(token); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', hash); setEnv('ADMIN_TOKEN', token); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that Run returns an error containing 'admin-email' when first boot is detected and AdminEmail is empty.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains zero rows
test_cases.expected: Run returns a non-nil error whose message contains 'admin-email' (specifically 'first boot: --admin-email is required')
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; assert strings.Contains(err.Error(), 'admin-email')

test_cases.description: Verify that Run stores the provided AdminEmail in admin_config under key 'admin_email' using INSERT OR REPLACE on first boot.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains zero rows
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
test_cases.expected: admin_config table contains a row with key='admin_email' and value='admin@example.com'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; v = queryAdminConfig(db, 'admin_email'); assert v == 'admin@example.com'

test_cases.description: Verify that the generated admin token consists of the TokenPrefix, '_admin_', and exactly 64 lowercase hex characters derived from 32 random bytes.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains zero rows
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
test_cases.expected: Token written to admin_token file matches regex '^ak_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; content = readFile(tmpDir + '/admin_token'); assert matches(content, '^ak_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$')

test_cases.description: Verify that the SHA-256 hash of the generated token (hex-encoded, 64 chars) is stored in admin_config under key 'admin_token_hash' on first boot.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains zero rows
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
test_cases.expected: admin_config contains key 'admin_token_hash' whose value equals hex(SHA256(token_file_contents)) and is exactly 64 lowercase hex characters
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; token = readFile(tmpDir + '/admin_token'); expectedHash = hexSHA256(token); storedHash = queryAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash'); assert storedHash == expectedHash; assert len(storedHash) == 64; assert isLowercaseHex(storedHash)

test_cases.description: Verify that Run writes the plaintext token to the admin_token file at the correct path with file mode 0600 on first boot.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains zero rows
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
XDG_CONFIG_HOME is unset (or set to a controlled test value)
test_cases.expected: File admin_token exists at ConfigDir/admin_token with mode 0600 and contains the plaintext token
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; fi = os.Stat(tmpDir + '/admin_token'); assert fi.Mode() == 0o600; assert len(readFile(tmpDir + '/admin_token')) > 0

test_cases.description: Verify that Run logs the absolute path of the admin_token file at warn level after writing it on first boot.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains zero rows
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
Logger is a test logger that captures log entries
test_cases.expected: A log entry at warn level exists whose message contains the absolute path of the admin_token file
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); hook = newLogCapture(); logger = newLoggerWithHook(hook); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: logger}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; warnEntries = hook.EntriesAtLevel(WARN); assert any(entry, strings.Contains(entry.Message, absPath(tmpDir + '/admin_token')))

test_cases.description: Verify that Run returns nil when the full first boot sequence completes without error.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains zero rows
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
Valid AdminEmail provided
test_cases.expected: Run returns nil error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that the admin_token file contains the raw plaintext token with no trailing newline.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains zero rows
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
test_cases.expected: File content does not end with '\n' or '\r\n' and contains a non-empty token string
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; content = readFileBytes(tmpDir + '/admin_token'); assert content[-1] != '\n'; assert len(content) > 0

test_cases.description: Verify that ShouldAutoPromote returns (true, nil) when the provided email matches the stored admin_email in admin_config.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
admin_config contains key 'admin_email' with value 'admin@example.com'
test_cases.expected: ShouldAutoPromote returns (true, nil)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_email', 'admin@example.com'); result, err = ShouldAutoPromote(ctx, db, 'admin@example.com'); assert err == nil; assert result == true

test_cases.description: Verify that ShouldAutoPromote returns (false, nil) when the provided email does not match the stored admin_email.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
admin_config contains key 'admin_email' with value 'admin@example.com'
test_cases.expected: ShouldAutoPromote returns (false, nil)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_email', 'admin@example.com'); result, err = ShouldAutoPromote(ctx, db, 'other@example.com'); assert err == nil; assert result == false

test_cases.description: Verify that ShouldAutoPromote returns (false, nil) when no admin_email key exists in admin_config.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
admin_config table is empty (no admin_email key)
test_cases.expected: ShouldAutoPromote returns (false, nil) without error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); result, err = ShouldAutoPromote(ctx, db, 'anyone@example.com'); assert err == nil; assert result == false

test_cases.description: Verify that ShouldAutoPromote is documented and designed to be called only for newly created users, not for updates to existing users.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
admin_config contains key 'admin_email' with value 'admin@example.com'
test_cases.expected: ShouldAutoPromote returns (true, nil) when called for a new user; callers must not call it for existing user updates (enforced by caller convention, not by this function itself)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_email', 'admin@example.com'); result, err = ShouldAutoPromote(ctx, db, 'admin@example.com'); assert err == nil; assert result == true; // The function itself does not check is_new_user; the OAuth caller is responsible for this gate

test_cases.description: Verify that the server logs the auto-promotion event at info level when ShouldAutoPromote returns true and the OAuth callback grants the admin role.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
admin_config contains key 'admin_email' with value 'admin@example.com'
Logger captures log entries
Caller (simulating OAuth callback) logs the auto-promotion event at info level upon receiving true from ShouldAutoPromote
test_cases.expected: An info-level log entry exists containing the promoted user's email
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_email', 'admin@example.com'); hook = newLogCapture(); logger = newLoggerWithHook(hook); result, err = ShouldAutoPromote(ctx, db, 'admin@example.com'); assert result == true; assert err == nil; logger.Infof('auto-promoted user %s to admin', 'admin@example.com'); infoEntries = hook.EntriesAtLevel(INFO); assert any(entry, strings.Contains(entry.Message, 'admin@example.com'))

test_cases.description: Verify that Run returns an error containing 'admin_token file exists' when the admin_token file is present on disk during a subsequent boot.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
admin_token file exists in ConfigDir
admin_config contains a valid admin_token_hash
test_cases.expected: Run returns a non-nil error whose message matches 'admin_token file exists at <path>: save the token securely and delete the file before restarting'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); writeFile(tmpDir + '/admin_token', 'sometoken', 0o600); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', hexSHA256('sometoken')); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; assert strings.Contains(err.Error(), 'admin_token file exists')

test_cases.description: Verify that Run returns an error containing 'ADMIN_TOKEN' when the ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable is empty or unset on a subsequent boot.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
admin_token file does not exist in ConfigDir
ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable is unset
admin_config contains a valid admin_token_hash
test_cases.expected: Run returns a non-nil error with message 'ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable is required'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); unsetEnv('ADMIN_TOKEN'); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', 'somehash'); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; assert strings.Contains(err.Error(), 'ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable is required')

test_cases.description: Verify that Run computes SHA-256 of ADMIN_TOKEN, hex-encodes it, and compares against admin_token_hash using subtle.ConstantTimeCompare on a subsequent boot.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
admin_token file does not exist
ADMIN_TOKEN is set to a known token
admin_config contains admin_token_hash = hex(SHA256(ADMIN_TOKEN))
test_cases.expected: Run returns nil (comparison passes) demonstrating it correctly compared SHA-256 hash of ADMIN_TOKEN against stored hash
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); token = 'ak_admin_' + hex(bytes(32)); hash = hexSHA256(token); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', hash); setEnv('ADMIN_TOKEN', token); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that Run returns an error containing 'does not match' when the SHA-256 hash of ADMIN_TOKEN does not match the stored admin_token_hash.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
admin_token file does not exist
ADMIN_TOKEN is set to a wrong token
admin_config contains a different admin_token_hash
test_cases.expected: Run returns a non-nil error with message 'ADMIN_TOKEN does not match the stored admin token'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); correctToken = 'ak_admin_' + hex(randomBytes(32)); wrongToken = 'ak_admin_' + hex(randomBytes(32)); assert correctToken != wrongToken; setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', hexSHA256(correctToken)); setEnv('ADMIN_TOKEN', wrongToken); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; assert strings.Contains(err.Error(), 'does not match')

test_cases.description: Verify that Run silently ignores AdminEmail on subsequent boot without logging a warning or modifying the stored admin_email.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
admin_config contains 'admin_email'='original@example.com' and a valid admin_token_hash
admin_token file does not exist
ADMIN_TOKEN env var set to the correct token
Logger captures log entries
test_cases.expected: Run returns nil; admin_email in admin_config remains 'original@example.com'; no warning log about admin_email
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); token = 'ak_admin_' + hex(randomBytes(32)); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', hexSHA256(token)); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_email', 'original@example.com'); setEnv('ADMIN_TOKEN', token); hook = newLogCapture(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'new@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLoggerWithHook(hook)}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; v = queryAdminConfig(db, 'admin_email'); assert v == 'original@example.com'

test_cases.description: Verify that Run returns nil when the subsequent boot hash comparison succeeds.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
admin_token file does not exist
ADMIN_TOKEN env var set to a token whose SHA-256 matches admin_token_hash in admin_config
test_cases.expected: Run returns nil
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); token = 'ak_admin_' + hex(randomBytes(32)); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', hexSHA256(token)); setEnv('ADMIN_TOKEN', token); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that Run returns an error instructing the operator to run with --reset-admin-token when admin_token_hash is absent from admin_config on a subsequent boot.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
admin_token file does not exist
admin_config does not contain the key 'admin_token_hash'
ADMIN_TOKEN env var is set
test_cases.expected: Run returns a non-nil error with message 'no admin token hash found in database; run with --reset-admin-token to generate a new token'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); setEnv('ADMIN_TOKEN', 'ak_admin_whatever'); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; assert strings.Contains(err.Error(), 'no admin token hash found')

test_cases.description: Verify that Run runs token rotation and generates a new admin token when ResetToken is true, regardless of user count.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row (subsequent boot context)
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
test_cases.expected: Run returns nil and a new admin_token file is written to ConfigDir containing a token matching '^<prefix>_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: true, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; content = readFile(tmpDir + '/admin_token'); assert matches(content, '^ak_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$')

test_cases.description: Verify that Run overwrites admin_token_hash in admin_config with the SHA-256 hash of the new token during token rotation, invalidating the old token.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
admin_config contains an existing admin_token_hash
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
test_cases.expected: admin_config.admin_token_hash is updated to the SHA-256 hash of the new token, which differs from the old hash
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); oldToken = 'ak_admin_' + hex(randomBytes(32)); oldHash = hexSHA256(oldToken); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', oldHash); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: true, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; newToken = readFile(tmpDir + '/admin_token'); newHash = queryAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash'); assert newHash == hexSHA256(newToken); assert newHash != oldHash

test_cases.description: Verify that Run writes the new plaintext token to the admin_token file with mode 0600 during token rotation.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
test_cases.expected: admin_token file exists at ConfigDir/admin_token with mode 0600 and contains a valid token format
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: true, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; fi = os.Stat(tmpDir + '/admin_token'); assert fi.Mode() == 0o600; content = readFile(tmpDir + '/admin_token'); assert matches(content, '^ak_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$')

test_cases.description: Verify that Run logs the admin_token file path at warn level after writing the token file during token rotation.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
Logger captures log entries
test_cases.expected: A warn-level log entry exists containing the absolute path of the admin_token file
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); hook = newLogCapture(); logger = newLoggerWithHook(hook); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: true, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: logger}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; warnEntries = hook.EntriesAtLevel(WARN); assert any(entry, strings.Contains(entry.Message, absPath(tmpDir + '/admin_token')))

test_cases.description: Verify that Run skips the file-presence guard and ADMIN_TOKEN env var check during a reset boot, returning nil even if the token file exists and ADMIN_TOKEN is unset.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
ADMIN_TOKEN environment variable is unset
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
test_cases.expected: Run returns nil without checking for the token file or ADMIN_TOKEN env var
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); unsetEnv('ADMIN_TOKEN'); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: true, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that on the next restart after token rotation (without --reset-admin-token), the file-presence guard and ADMIN_TOKEN validation apply normally.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table contains at least one row
A previous token rotation boot left the admin_token file on disk
ADMIN_TOKEN env var is unset
test_cases.expected: Run returns a non-nil error because the admin_token file is present (file-presence guard fires)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); writeFile(tmpDir + '/admin_token', 'ak_admin_abc', 0o600); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', hexSHA256('ak_admin_abc')); unsetEnv('ADMIN_TOKEN'); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; assert strings.Contains(err.Error(), 'admin_token file exists')

test_cases.description: Verify that the token generation function produces tokens strictly in the format '<prefix>_admin_<64 lowercase hex chars>'.
preconditions: No database required — testing the token generation helper in isolation
test_cases.expected: Generated token matches regex '^ak_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$' and the hex portion is exactly 64 characters
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: token = generateToken('ak'); assert matches(token, '^ak_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$'); parts = strings.Split(token, '_admin_'); assert len(parts) == 2; assert len(parts[1]) == 64; assert isLowercaseHex(parts[1])

test_cases.description: Verify that token hash computation uses SHA-256 over the full token string and produces a 64-character lowercase hex digest.
preconditions: No database required
test_cases.expected: hashToken(token) returns a 64-character lowercase hex string equal to hex(SHA256(token))
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: token = 'ak_admin_aabbccddeeff00112233445566778899aabbccddeeff00112233445566778899'; expected = hexSHA256(token); result = hashToken(token); assert result == expected; assert len(result) == 64; assert isLowercaseHex(result)

test_cases.description: Verify that token hash comparison uses subtle.ConstantTimeCompare by checking correct results for matching and non-matching inputs.
preconditions: No database required
test_cases.expected: compareHashes(hashToken(token_a), hashToken(token_a)) returns true; compareHashes(hashToken(token_a), hashToken(token_b)) returns false
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: hashA = hexSHA256('ak_admin_aaaa'); hashB = hexSHA256('ak_admin_bbbb'); assert compareTokenHashes(hashA, hashA) == true; assert compareTokenHashes(hashA, hashB) == false; // The implementation must use subtle.ConstantTimeCompare — verify by code inspection or grep for 'ConstantTimeCompare' in bootstrap.go

test_cases.description: Verify that the token generator never uses math/rand by inspecting the source or confirming crypto/rand is used.
preconditions: Source file internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go is available
test_cases.expected: Source file does not import 'math/rand' and does import 'crypto/rand'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: source = readFile('internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go'); assert not strings.Contains(source, '"math/rand"'); assert strings.Contains(source, '"crypto/rand"')

test_cases.description: Verify that the admin_token file contains only the raw plaintext token with no trailing newline.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table is empty (first boot)
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
test_cases.expected: readFileBytes(admin_token) does not end with 0x0A (newline); content is a valid token string
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; Run(ctx, params); raw = readFileBytes(tmpDir + '/admin_token'); assert raw[-1] != 0x0A; assert matches(string(raw), '^ak_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$')

test_cases.description: Verify that Run is invoked only after LoadConfig and db.Open complete, and that it returns before the HTTP server starts.
preconditions: Full server startup sequence observable (e.g., via integration test or startup ordering test)
In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table is empty
test_cases.expected: Run completes and returns nil before any HTTP listener binds; DB schema exists when Run is called
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); // schema must exist before Run; assert tableExists(db, 'users'); assert tableExists(db, 'admin_config'); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; // HTTP server is only started after Run returns nil in main()

test_cases.description: Verify that Run executes token rotation first and returns without running first-boot or subsequent-boot sequences when ResetToken is true.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table may be empty or non-empty
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
test_cases.expected: Run returns nil after writing a new token file; first-boot and subsequent-boot checks are not executed
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); // Test with zero users — first boot checks would require AdminEmail; ResetToken=true should bypass that params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: true, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; // If first-boot ran, it would error because AdminEmail is empty; since it doesn't, rotation took priority

test_cases.description: Verify that when Run returns a non-nil error, the server binary's main() treats it as fatal and must not start the HTTP server.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database opened with schema applied
users table is empty, AdminEmail is empty (guaranteed error condition)
test_cases.expected: Run returns non-nil error; the caller is expected to call log.Fatal(err) and not proceed to start the HTTP listener
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; // Verify Run does not itself call os.Exit or log.Fatal (no panic, no process termination observed); // The test process continues after Run returns

test_cases.description: Verify that the Run function documentation and design acknowledge the single-instance startup constraint (concurrent calls are not supported).
preconditions: Source file internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go is available
test_cases.expected: The godoc comment for Run mentions that concurrent calls against the same database are not supported
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: source = readFile('internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go'); assert strings.Contains(source, 'Concurrent') OR strings.Contains(source, 'single-instance'); // Additionally, sequential calls in a single test complete without deadlock or panic: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); token = 'ak_admin_' + hex(randomBytes(32)); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', hexSHA256(token)); setEnv('ADMIN_TOKEN', token); params = {DB: db, ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil

test_cases.description: Verify that Run resolves the admin_token file path to $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/apikit/admin_token when XDG_CONFIG_HOME is set.
preconditions: XDG_CONFIG_HOME environment variable is set to a temp directory
ConfigDir is set to '$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/apikit'
In-memory SQLite database with zero users
test_cases.expected: admin_token file is created at /tmp/xdgtest/apikit/admin_token
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: setEnv('XDG_CONFIG_HOME', '/tmp/xdgtest'); db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: '/tmp/xdgtest/apikit', TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; fi = os.Stat('/tmp/xdgtest/apikit/admin_token'); assert fi != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that Run resolves the admin_token file path to ./admin_token (ConfigDir/admin_token) when XDG_CONFIG_HOME is not set.
preconditions: XDG_CONFIG_HOME environment variable is unset
ConfigDir is set to a writable temp directory representing the current working directory
In-memory SQLite database with zero users
test_cases.expected: admin_token file is created at /tmp/cwdtest/admin_token
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: unsetEnv('XDG_CONFIG_HOME'); db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: '/tmp/cwdtest', TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; fi = os.Stat('/tmp/cwdtest/admin_token'); assert fi != nil

test_cases.description: Verify that Run uses the ConfigDir field of BootstrapParams to construct the absolute path to the admin_token file.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database with zero users
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory with a known absolute path
test_cases.expected: admin_token file is created at exactly ConfigDir + '/admin_token'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); configDir = '/tmp/configdirtest'; os.MkdirAll(configDir); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: configDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err == nil; assert os.Stat(configDir + '/admin_token') != nil

property_tests.description: For any two independently generated admin tokens, they must be different with overwhelming probability.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate pairs of tokens using the internal token generation function with any valid TokenPrefix string
property_tests.invariant_check: token1 = generateToken(prefix); token2 = generateToken(prefix); assert token1 != token2

property_tests.description: For any admin token string, hashing it twice with SHA-256 and hex-encoding must produce the same 64-character lowercase hex result.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any non-empty string token (using Go's testing/quick or manual table of representative tokens including various prefixes and lengths)
property_tests.invariant_check: hash1 = hexSHA256(token); hash2 = hexSHA256(token); assert hash1 == hash2; assert len(hash1) == 64; assert isLowercaseHex(hash1)

property_tests.description: For any two distinct admin token strings, their SHA-256 hex digests must be different, ensuring the wrong token never passes hash comparison.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Pairs of distinct non-empty strings (token_a, token_b) where token_a != token_b
property_tests.invariant_check: if token_a != token_b: assert hexSHA256(token_a) != hexSHA256(token_b)

property_tests.description: For any subsequent boot attempt where the admin_token file is present on disk, Run must always return a non-nil error.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any valid admin_config state (with a stored hash), any token file content, any ADMIN_TOKEN env var value
property_tests.invariant_check: writeFile(configDir + '/admin_token', anyContent); err = Run(ctx, params_with_users_and_no_reset); assert err != nil; assert strings.Contains(err.Error(), 'admin_token file exists')

property_tests.description: For any token rotation that stores a new admin_token_hash, the SHA-256 hash of the old token must no longer match the stored hash.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any prior admin token and any database state with users
property_tests.invariant_check: oldToken = generateToken('ak'); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', hexSHA256(oldToken)); Run(ctx, {ResetToken: true, ...}); newHash = queryAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash'); assert subtle.ConstantTimeCompare([]byte(hexSHA256(oldToken)), []byte(newHash)) == 0

property_tests.description: For any first boot attempt with an empty AdminEmail, Run must return a non-nil error and neither admin_email nor admin_token_hash must be written to admin_config.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any empty AdminEmail value (empty string), any database state with zero users
property_tests.invariant_check: db = openMemoryDB(); Run(ctx, {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ...}); assert queryAdminConfig(db, 'admin_email') == ''; assert queryAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash') == ''

property_tests.description: For any call to ShouldAutoPromote when no admin_email key exists in admin_config, it must return (false, nil) regardless of the email argument.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any non-empty email string, with admin_config table present but empty (no rows)
property_tests.invariant_check: db = openMemoryDB(); // no admin_email in admin_config; result, err = ShouldAutoPromote(ctx, db, anyEmail); assert result == false; assert err == nil

property_tests.description: For any error condition encountered inside Run, it must signal failure by returning a non-nil error and must never call os.Exit or log.Fatal.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Any combination of invalid inputs (empty AdminEmail on first boot, wrong ADMIN_TOKEN, file permission errors, database errors)
property_tests.invariant_check: // Static check: source = readFile('internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go'); assert not strings.Contains(source, 'os.Exit'); assert not strings.Contains(source, 'log.Fatal'); // Dynamic check: for each error scenario, err = Run(ctx, badParams); assert err != nil; // test process survives (no os.Exit was called)

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that Run returns a non-nil error wrapping a database error when SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users fails.
preconditions: A mock or broken SQLite database that returns an error for any query
edge_case_tests.expected: Run returns a non-nil error whose message wraps the underlying database error
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = openBrokenDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; assert errors.Unwrap(err) != nil OR strings.Contains(err.Error(), 'sql')

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that Run returns a non-nil error wrapping the crypto/rand error when random byte generation fails on first boot.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database with zero users
crypto/rand.Read is mocked/replaced to return an error
edge_case_tests.expected: Run returns a non-nil error; no admin_token_hash or admin_token file is written
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); // inject failing rand; params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = runWithBrokenRand(ctx, params); assert err != nil; assert queryAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash') == ''; assert not fileExists(tmpDir + '/admin_token')

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that Run returns a non-nil error wrapping the OS error when writing the admin_token file fails on first boot.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database with zero users
ConfigDir is a read-only directory or a path that cannot be written to
edge_case_tests.expected: Run returns a non-nil error wrapping an os.WriteFile or permission error; server does not start
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); readOnlyDir = createReadOnlyDir(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: readOnlyDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; assert errors.Unwrap(err) != nil OR strings.Contains(err.Error(), 'permission')

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that Run returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error when storing admin_email or admin_token_hash fails during first boot.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database with zero users
Database write operations are mocked to fail after user count query succeeds
edge_case_tests.expected: Run returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error; server does not start
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryWithWriteFailure(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that Run never calls os.Exit or log.Fatal internally; all errors are returned to the caller.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database with zero users
AdminEmail is empty (triggers a guaranteed error path)
edge_case_tests.expected: Run returns a non-nil error; the test process is not terminated; no os.Exit or log.Fatal is called
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; // If Run called os.Exit, this assertion would never execute — its execution proves Run returned normally; source = readFile('internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go'); assert not strings.Contains(source, 'os.Exit'); assert not strings.Contains(source, 'log.Fatal')

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that ShouldAutoPromote returns (false, error) wrapping the database error when the query to read admin_email fails.
preconditions: A broken SQLite database connection that returns an error on query
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = openBrokenDB(); result, err = ShouldAutoPromote(ctx, db, 'admin@example.com'); assert result == false; assert err != nil

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that Run returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error when reading admin_token_hash from admin_config fails during a subsequent boot.
preconditions: A database where the users table returns count > 0 but admin_config reads fail
admin_token file does not exist
ADMIN_TOKEN env var is set
edge_case_tests.expected: Run returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = openDBWithAdminConfigReadFailure(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; setEnv('ADMIN_TOKEN', 'some_token'); err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that the token hash comparison function uses subtle.ConstantTimeCompare to prevent timing side-channel attacks.
preconditions: Source file internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go is available
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: source = readFile('internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go'); assert strings.Contains(source, 'subtle.ConstantTimeCompare'); assert strings.Contains(source, '"crypto/subtle"'); // Functional check: compareTokenHashes(h, h) == true; compareTokenHashes(h, differentH) == false

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that Run returns a non-nil error wrapping the crypto/rand error during token rotation without modifying the old admin_token_hash.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database with at least one user
admin_config contains an existing admin_token_hash
crypto/rand.Read is mocked to return an error
edge_case_tests.expected: Run returns non-nil error; admin_token_hash in admin_config remains unchanged (equal to oldHash)
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); oldToken = 'ak_admin_' + hex(randomBytes(32)); oldHash = hexSHA256(oldToken); setAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash', oldHash); err = runWithBrokenRand(ctx, {DB: db, ResetToken: true, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}); assert err != nil; storedHash = queryAdminConfig(db, 'admin_token_hash'); assert storedHash == oldHash

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that Run returns a non-nil error wrapping the OS error when writing the admin_token file fails during token rotation.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database with at least one user
ConfigDir is a read-only directory
edge_case_tests.expected: Run returns a non-nil error wrapping an os.WriteFile error; server does not start
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); insertUser(db); readOnlyDir = createReadOnlyDir(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: true, ConfigDir: readOnlyDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; assert strings.Contains(err.Error(), 'permission') OR errors.Unwrap(err) != nil

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that Run returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error when updating admin_token_hash fails during token rotation.
preconditions: A database that allows reading (user count) but fails on admin_config writes during rotation
ConfigDir is a writable temp directory
edge_case_tests.expected: Run returns a non-nil error wrapping the database error; server does not start
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = openDBWithAdminConfigWriteFailure(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: true, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that Run never calls os.Exit or log.Fatal for any error condition by inspecting source and confirming test process survives all error paths.
preconditions: Source file internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go is available
edge_case_tests.expected: Source file does not contain 'os.Exit' or 'log.Fatal'; all error paths return non-nil error values
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: source = readFile('internal/bootstrap/bootstrap.go'); assert not strings.Contains(source, 'os.Exit'); assert not strings.Contains(source, 'log.Fatal'); // Confirm multiple error paths all return normally: db = openMemoryDB(); err1 = Run(ctx, {DB: db, AdminEmail: '', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: tmpDir, TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}); assert err1 != nil; // test process is still running

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that Run returns a non-nil error wrapping an OS error when the directory for the admin_token file does not exist and cannot be created.
preconditions: In-memory SQLite database with zero users
ConfigDir is set to a path that does not exist and cannot be created (e.g., inside a read-only filesystem)
edge_case_tests.expected: Run returns a non-nil error wrapping an OS error; admin_token file is not created
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: db = openMemoryDB(); params = {DB: db, AdminEmail: 'admin@example.com', ResetToken: false, ConfigDir: '/nonexistent/readonly/path', TokenPrefix: 'ak', Logger: newLogger()}; err = Run(ctx, params); assert err != nil; assert not fileExists('/nonexistent/readonly/path/admin_token')

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the happy-path first boot: operator starts a fresh server with --admin-email, token and hash are stored, token file is written, server proceeds normally.
smoke_tests.trigger: Call Run with zero users in the DB and a valid AdminEmail
expected_effects: admin_config.admin_email = 'admin@example.com'
admin_config.admin_token_hash is a 64-char lowercase hex string equal to hex(SHA256(token_file_contents))
admin_token file exists at ConfigDir/admin_token with mode 0600
admin_token file contains a token matching '^ak_admin_[0-9a-f]{64}$' with no trailing newline
A warn-level log entry contains the absolute path of the admin_token file
Run returns nil

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the subsequent boot happy path: operator deletes the token file, sets ADMIN_TOKEN env var, and restarts; Run validates the token and returns nil.
smoke_tests.trigger: Call Run with users in the DB, no token file, and ADMIN_TOKEN set to the matching token
expected_effects: No new token file is created
admin_config is not modified
Run returns nil

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the file-presence guard: operator restarts without deleting the admin_token file; Run returns an error blocking server startup.
smoke_tests.trigger: Call Run with users in the DB and the admin_token file present in ConfigDir
expected_effects: Run returns a non-nil error containing 'admin_token file exists at <path>'
HTTP server is not started (caller receives non-nil error)

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for token rotation: operator restarts with --reset-admin-token; Run generates a new token, invalidates the old hash, writes the new token file, and returns nil.
smoke_tests.trigger: Call Run with ResetToken=true, users in the DB, and an existing admin_token_hash in admin_config
expected_effects: admin_config.admin_token_hash is updated to a new value different from the previous hash
admin_token file exists at ConfigDir/admin_token with mode 0600 containing the new token
A warn-level log entry contains the absolute path of the admin_token file
Run returns nil
Old token no longer matches stored hash

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for auto-promotion: a user with the designated admin email authenticates via OAuth for the first time; ShouldAutoPromote returns true.
smoke_tests.trigger: Call ShouldAutoPromote with the email matching admin_config.admin_email
expected_effects: ShouldAutoPromote returns (true, nil)
Caller (simulated OAuth handler) logs info-level auto-promotion event containing the user's email

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test covering first boot, auto-promotion on first OAuth login, and subsequent boot with ADMIN_TOKEN validation.
smoke_tests.trigger: Sequentially: (1) Run with zero users and AdminEmail, (2) ShouldAutoPromote with matching email, (3) Run with users and valid ADMIN_TOKEN after deleting token file
expected_effects: After step 1: admin_email and admin_token_hash stored; admin_token file written with mode 0600; Run returns nil
After step 2: ShouldAutoPromote returns (true, nil) for the designated email; info-level log entry for auto-promotion
After step 3 (token file deleted, ADMIN_TOKEN set): Run returns nil; system is fully operational
